name: Ali Baba Tails
description:
Sonic and the Secret Rings (ソニックと秘密のリング Sonikku to Himitsu no Ringu?) is a video game in the Sonic the Hedgehog series developed by Sonic Team and published by Sega. It was released for the Wii on 20 February 2007 in North America, 2 March 2007 in Europe, and 15 March 2007 in Japan. It is the first installment in the Sonic Storybook Series.

  • Ali Baba is first seen in the Sand Oasis stage after Sonic saves him from being attacked by Erazor's henchman. His purpose in the story is small, he advises on various points in the game and tells Sonic where to find Sinbad. He takes on the appearance of Miles "Tails" Prower.

Ali Baba: A Two-Tailed Fox in Medieval Persia

In the bustling heart of a 10th-century Persian town, nestled among winding streets fragrant with saffron and jasmine, lives Ali Baba, a young, two-tailed fox with an irrepressible "can do" spirit and an unmistakable youthful charm. Unlike the ordinary woodcutters of his time, Ali Baba’s twin tails—vibrant orange and tipped with white—twirl like rotary blades, granting him the ability to hover above the dusty bazaars or glide over the rugged Zagros Mountains. His large, expressive eyes and high-pitched, boyish voice make him undeniably adorable, though he bristles at the notion, insisting, “I’m not cute! I’m a serious inventor!” His small, agile frame belies a boundless imagination, and his fur, dusted with the sand of his woodcutting treks, glows under the Persian sun.

Ali Baba resides in a modest mud-brick home, its courtyard shaded by a pomegranate tree and filled with the scent of flatbread baking on a clay oven. The house, simple yet cozy, reflects the humble life of a woodcutter in medieval Persia, with woven rugs on the floor and copper lanterns casting warm light. Yet, within this unassuming dwelling lies Ali Baba’s workshop, a cluttered nook brimming with his ingenious creations—clockwork contraptions, pulley-driven carts, and even a miniature waterwheel powered by a nearby stream, all crafted from wood, bronze, and scraps scavenged from the bazaar. He tinkers late into the night with his inventions, his twin tails twitching with excitement as he dreams of machines that could rival the wonders of Baghdad’s scholars

Culturally, Ali Baba is steeped in the traditions of his Persian town. He joins neighbors
